basic_details.job_title: West Bengal Postal Circle Recruitment 2026 through LDCE - Apply Offline for 85 Postal Assistant, Sorting Assistant Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Office of the Chief Postmaster General, West Bengal Circle, Kolkata – 700 012
basic_details.post_name: Postal Assistant (Circle Office & Regional Offices), Postal Assistant (Post Office), Sorting Assistant (Railway Mail Service)
basic_details.department: Department of Posts, Ministry of Communications, Government of India
basic_details.total_vacancies: 85 (Tentative: PA Circle/RO – 4, PA Post Office – 44, SA RMS – 37)
basic_details.short_information: India Post West Bengal Circle Postal Assistant Sorting Assistant Recruitment 2026: Get the latest India Post West Bengal Circle recruitment notification 2026 for 85 Postal Assistant and Sorting Assistant vacancies through Limited Departmental Competitive Examination (LDCE) for promotion from eligible officials for the vacancy year 2026 (01.01.2026 to 31.12.2026). The last date to submit application forms at Divisional Office is 29th May 2026 and the date of examination is 21st June 2026. Find India Post WB Circle vacancies on the official website www.westbengalpost.gov.in. You can also get Fre

qualification.details: As per the Department of Posts (Postal Assistant & Sorting Assistant) Recruitment Rules 2022 (notified vide GSR 459(E) dated 17.06.2022 as amended), the following categories of officials of the Circle, excluding officials from Postal Account Office or Wing, are eligible to appear in the examination. Eligibility of service condition shall be as on 01.01.2026. Eligibility shall be determined with respect to the post held substantively and relevant pay level — not based on pay drawn due to financial upgradation under TBOP, BCR, or MACP. | Category (A): | Category (B): | Note: Category (B) candidates will be considered only if sufficient Category (A) officials are not available for filling the notified vacancy.

vacancy_details.Name of Cadre: Postal Assistant (Circle Office and Regional Offices)

selection_process: Paper Type Maximum Marks Duration Language
Paper-I Competitive (MCQ) 100 (in 2 Parts) 120 Minutes Bilingual (Hindi and English)
Paper-III (DEST) Qualifying (Data Entry Skill Test on Computer) 25 15 Minutes On Computer
General / OBC / EWS 40% in each paper 75%
PWD (if not exempted for skill test) 33% in each paper 60%
Merit Basis Order of marks secured in Paper-I only. Paper-III (DEST) is only qualifying — marks not added to Paper-I for merit.
DEST Shortlisting Candidates to be shortlisted for DEST shall be 3 times the number of vacancies declared at the time of notification.
VH Candidates Exempted from appearing in DEST (applicable from recruitment year 2022 onwards).

The selection process is based on a Written Examination as per the revised pattern and syllabus circulated by Directorate vide letter No. 17-08/2018-SPB-I dated 10.05.2019 and subsequent amendments enclosed as Annexure-II. The Data Entry Skill Test (DEST) will be conducted subsequently as per instructions on the subject mentioned in Directorate’s letter No. 17-08/2018-SPN-I dated 19.12.2022. The local language test component (Paper-II) has been removed vide letter No. 17-08/2018-SPN-I dated 29.05.2024.
Written Examination Pattern (Annexure-C – LDCE for Postal Assistant/Sorting Assistant)

syllabus.details: The Written Examination consists of Paper-I (120 Minutes, 100 marks in 2 parts) as per the revised LDCE syllabus for Postal Assistant/Sorting Assistant. All questions are MCQ type. The examination will be conducted without the aid of books. | Paper-I (120 Minutes, Full Marks: 100) | Paper-III – Data Entry Skill Test / DEST (15 Minutes, 25 Marks)

how_to_apply: Applications must be submitted in the prescribed format (Annexure-I) through the Divisional Office/Controlling Unit. No direct or online applications are accepted. The APS candidates should send their applications to the concerned Divisions/Units only. Candidates must ensure they fulfill/satisfy all eligibility criteria and conditions before submitting the application.

Candidates applying must indicate their order of preference for Cadre and then Division/Unit as per Annexure-III. Allotment of Cadre/Division/Unit will be made on merit-cum-preference basis subject to availability of vacancy. Candidates should indicate preference only for Divisions/Units under West Bengal Circle as per notified vacancies.
Application must be sent to: The Assistant Director of Postal Services (Rectt.), Office of the Chief Postmaster General, West Bengal Circle, Kolkata – 700 012
important_instructions: Only eligible officials of West Bengal Circle (excluding Postal Account Office/Wing) may apply; eligibility is as on 01.01.2026. Applications must be submitted in the prescribed format (Annexure-I) through Divisional Office/Controlling Unit — no direct or online applications will be accepted. Category (B) candidates will be considered only if sufficient Category (A) officials are not available. Eligibility shall be determined with respect to the post held substantively and not based on financial upgradation under TBOP/BCR/MACP. APS candidates should send their applications to the concerned Divisions/Units only. Candidates must indicate cadre and division/unit preference (Annexure-III); allotment will be on merit-cum-preference basis. The local language test (Paper-II) has been removed; only Paper-I (Written) and Paper-III (DEST) are applicable. VH (Visually Handicapped) candidates are exempted from appearing in DEST. Examination will commence at 10:00 AM; candidates must appear from their respective Region’s centre only. All instructions issued by Directorate regarding conduct of examination, SOP, disciplinary proceedings, and scribe/compensatory time shall be followed scrupulously. The Competent Authority reserves the right to cancel, change, or modify the schedule, pattern, and syllabus at any point of time. This notification is available on www.indiapost.gov.in and www.westbengalpost.gov.in.

faq.question: Q1. How many vacancies are there in India Post West Bengal Circle LDCE Postal Assistant & Sorting Assistant Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: India Post West Bengal Circle has notified 85 tentative vacancies for the posts of Postal Assistant (CO/RO & Post Office) and Sorting Assistant (RMS) for the vacancy year 2026 (01.01.2026 to 31.12.2026).

faq.question: Q2. Who is eligible to apply for India Post WB Circle LDCE Postal Assistant & Sorting Assistant 2026?
faq.answer: Only eligible Departmental officials of West Bengal Circle (excluding Postal Accounts Office/Wing) who meet the service conditions as on 01.01.2026 can apply for this LDCE; it is not open to outsiders or GDS of other circles.

faq.question: Q3. What are the important dates for India Post West Bengal Circle LDCE Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit applications at Divisional Office is 29 May 2026, applications must reach Regional Office by 04 June 2026, Circle Office by 09 June 2026, and the LDCE exam will be held on 21 June 2026 at 10:00 AM.

faq.question: Q4. What posts are filled through India Post WB Circle LDCE 2026?
faq.answer: This LDCE fills promotional vacancies for Postal Assistant (Circle Office & Regional Offices), Postal Assistant (Post Office) and Sorting Assistant (Railway Mail Service) under West Bengal Circle for the vacancy year 2026.

faq.question: Q5. What is the exam pattern for India Post West Bengal Circle LDCE Postal Assistant & Sorting Assistant 2026?
faq.answer: The selection is based on a Written Examination (Paper‑I, MCQ, 100 marks, 120 minutes) followed by a Data Entry Skill Test (DEST – Paper‑III, 25 marks, 15 minutes), conducted as per the revised LDCE pattern; the earlier local language Paper‑II has been removed.

faq.question: Q6. What are the minimum qualifying marks in India Post WB Circle LDCE 2026?
faq.answer: Minimum qualifying marks in Paper‑I are 40% for General/OBC/EWS and 33% for SC/ST/PwBD, while in DEST (Paper‑III) candidates must secure 75% for General/OBC/EWS and 60% for SC/ST/PwBD, where applicable.

faq.question: Q7. How are Category A and Category B officials defined for India Post WB LDCE 2026?
faq.answer: Category A includes officials in Level‑3 with 3 years’ service, Level‑2 with 5 years’ service (including Level‑1) or Level‑1 with 5 years’ service. Category B includes officials in Level‑1/2/3 with 8 years’ total regular service including regular GDS service (5 years if regular GDS service existed on or before 17.06.2022) and is considered only if sufficient Category A candidates are not available.

faq.question: Q8. How is merit prepared for India Post WB Circle LDCE Postal Assistant & Sorting Assistant 2026?
faq.answer: The final merit list is prepared purely on marks obtained in Paper‑I, subject to candidates qualifying the DEST; DEST marks are not added to Paper‑I, and cadre/division allocation is done on a merit‑cum‑preference basis as per options filled in Annexure‑III.
